belikrk-eggplant-kebab-lezzet.name: Eggplant Kebab
instructions: 1. Wash and peel off eggplants.
2. Draw the finger thickness.
3. Wait for 10 minutes in salted water, wash with plenty of water and drain.
4. Clean onions for internal material and shore.
5. Wash and repel one of the tomatoes.
6. Mix mint, salt and spices in a deep bowl.
7. Prepare the meatballs from the mixture and rolling the pieces.
8. A slice of eggplants and meatballs in the pot, respectively, index materials side by side.
9. Place three tomatoes sliced on the bases.
10. Bake 30-35 minutes in the oven with a pre-heated 170 degree.
11. 11. Serve by decorating fresh mint leaves.
12. Check out the classic eggplant kebab recipe here.

ear-feijoada-recipe-teleculinaria.name: Ear Feijoada Recipe
ear-feijoada-recipe-teleculinaria.original_name: Receita fácil de Feijoada de orelheira
instructions: Clean and wash the earpiece and cook it in water, along with the sausage and sausage. Remove the meat as it is cooked, and reserve the broth from the cooking. Let it cool, remove the bones from the earpiece and cut the meat into pieces.

Peel and mince the onions and garlic, lay them in a pot with the olive oil, take it to the fire and let the onions become soft. Add the blonde, carrot sliced and tomato chopped with the sauce and let cook for 5 minutes, stirring from time to time.
De seguida, acrescente a orelheira, tempere com sal, pimenta e colorau, adicione 500 ml do caldo das carnes e deixe levantar fervura.
Add the baked beans, stir well and let it clear. Remove from the fire, add the sausage and sausage and serve sprinkled with chopped parsley.

ear-salad-in-vinaigrette-teleculinaria.name: Ear Salad In Vinaigrette
instructions: Cook your ear in a pot with water and salt for about 40 minutes. Remove, drain and let it cool.
Peel and cut the onion into cubes; peel also the garlic and pick them; wash the pepper, eliminate its seeds and cut it into cubes, the same size as the onion.
In a bowl, mix well with a rod of wires to mustard, oil, vinegar, salt and spicy powder.
Cut the ear into pieces and add it to the previous sauce. Add the previously washed and chosen cress, the onion, garlic and pepper. Stir and follow with tomato cut into slices.

earl-grey-hot-toddy.name: Earl Grey Hot Toddy
instructions: Bring water to boil in a pot, then add the tea bags. Steep at a low temperature for 5 minutes.
Add the lemon juice, honey, and whiskey and simmer for 5 more minutes.
Enjoy!

earl-grey-martini.name: Earl Grey Martini
instructions: Place 10 oz. vodka and 4 Earl Grey tea bags in a small bowl, making sure tea bags are submerged. Cover and let sit at room temperature 5 minutes (vodka should turn a smoky quartz hue and smell fragrant). Uncover, remove tea bags, and squeeze over bowl to release all of the vodka; discard.

To make 1 cocktail, combine 2½ oz. infused vodka, ½ oz. vermouth, 1 large egg white, one 3x2" strip lemon zest, and 1 tsp. honey in a cocktail shaker. Stir until honey is dissolved, then fill halfway with ice. Cover and shake vigorously until outside of shaker is very frosty, 30–45 seconds. Strain into a chilled coupe glass.
Do Ahead: Vodka can be infused 1 month ahead. Transfer to an airtight container and chill.
*Raw egg is not recommended for the elderly, pregnant individuals, people with weakened immune systems…or people who just don’t like raw egg.

earl-grey-teabourbon-punch.name: Earl Grey Tea–bourbon Punch

ingredients.item: Loose earl grey tea or 4 bags

instructions: Combine tea and 1 1/4 cups boiling water in a heatproof bowl. Let steep 5 minutes, then strain through a fine-mesh sieve into a measuring glass (you should have 1 cup). Let cool.
Meanwhile, bring honey, thyme, 2 rosemary sprigs, and 1/2 cup water to a gentle simmer over medium heat, stirring to combine. Let cool; discard herbs.
Combine tea, honey syrup, lemon juice, bourbon, Cognac, and bitters in a large pitcher or punch bowl. Add 2 cups ice and stir until ice is melted and punch is very cold.
Serve punch in tumblers filled with ice, garnished with rosemary sprigs and lemon wheels.
Syrup and tea can be made 3 days ahead; cover separately and chill. Punch can be made 1 day ahead; cover and chill.

earl-grey-tiramisu.name: Earl Grey Tiramisu

ingredients.item: Grand marnier or other orange liqueur
. vanilla bean paste or vanilla extract
Diamond crystal or  0.25 tsp. morton kosher salt

instructions: Bring 1½ cups water to a boil in a medium saucepan. Remove from heat and immediately add 10 Earl Grey tea bags and 2 Tbsp. sugar. Let sit, stirring occasionally, 10 minutes.

Remove tea bags, wringing them out over pan; discard tea bags. Pour tea into a shallow bowl and stir in ⅓ cup Grand Marnier or other orange liqueur and a pinch of kosher salt. Let cool.
Do Ahead: Soak can be made 1 day ahead. Cover and chill.

Pour water into a clean medium saucepan to come 2" up sides and bring to a simmer. Combine 3 large egg yolks and 2 Tbsp. water in a medium heatproof bowl. Cut open 3 Earl Grey tea bags and empty into a spice mill or blender. Add ¹⁄₃ cup sugar and pulse until tea is very finely ground; add to bowl with egg yolks. Set bowl over saucepan (do not let it touch water) and reduce heat to low. Using an electric mixer (or a large whisk), beat mixture on medium-high speed until eggs are tripled in volume and pale yellow in color, about 5 minutes. Remove bowl from heat; let cool.

Using clean beaters, beat 1½ cups chilled heavy cream and ¾ cup mascarpone in a separate medium bowl on medium-high speed until medium peaks form. Add 1 tsp. vanilla bean paste or vanilla extract and ½ tsp. Diamond Crystal or ¼ tsp. Morton kosher salt and beat 1 minute. Add egg yolk mixture and gently fold with a rubber spatula just until no streaks remain.

Working one at a time, dip 10 crisp ladyfingers into soak, leaving just until slightly softened, 1–2 seconds per side. Arrange ladyfingers in an even layer in an 8x8" baking dish. Using an offset spatula, spread half of filling evenly over. Working one at a time, soak remaining 10 crisp ladyfingers and layer in baking dish over filling. Spread remaining filling over, creating swooshes and swirls as desired. Cover baking dish with plastic wrap and chill tea-ramisu at least 4 hours.

Just before serving, uncover tea-ramisu and lightly dust with unsweetened cocoa powder.
Do Ahead: Tea-ramisu (without cocoa powder) can be made 1 day ahead. Keep chilled.

earl-grey-yogurt-cake.name: Earl Grey Yogurt Cake

ingredients.item: Diamond crystal or 1 tsp. morton kosher salt
. loose-leaf earl grey tea or  0.25 cup tea from bags

instructions: Preheat oven to 325°. Lightly coat a 9x5" or 8½x4½" loaf pan with vegetable oil and line with parchment paper, leaving overhang on long sides. If you’re not sure what size your loaf pan is, measure it with a ruler from inside edge to inside edge for both the length and width. If you measure from the outside edge, you’ll get an inaccurate reading of the width of the pan, and your cake may end up overflowing! It's the actual empty space of the pan where the cake bakes that matters here, not the width of the entire pan including its edges.
Whisk 2 cups (250 g) all-purpose flour, 1½ tsp. Diamond Crystal or 1 tsp. Morton kosher salt, ½ tsp. baking powder, and ½ tsp. baking soda in a medium bowl to combine.

Vigorously whisk 2 large eggs and 1¼ cups (250 g) granulated sugar in a large bowl 1 minute (seriously, time it!); mixture should be pale yellow and frothy. Whisk in 1 cup plain whole-milk yogurt, 3 Tbsp. loose-leaf Earl Grey tea (or ¼ cup tea from bags), and 2 tsp. vanilla extract.

Gradually stream in 1 cup vegetable oil, whisking constantly until incorporated. (Place a kitchen towel under your bowl to stabilize it so that you have two free hands.) Add dry ingredients and whisk to combine. Scrape batter into prepared pan and smooth top. Gently tap pan against surface to eliminate any air bubbles.
Sprinkle evenly with 1 Tbsp. raw or granulated sugar. Bake cake until a skewer or toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean, about 1 hour.

Let cool 15 minutes in pan, then run a butter knife or offset spatula between the cake and pan to release. Lift it out using parchment overhang and transfer to a wire rack. Serve warm or room temperature (or even better, toast slices and slather them with butter!).

earl-greybourbon-punch.name: Earl Grey–bourbon Punch

ingredients.item: Loose earl grey tea or 4 bags

instructions: Combine tea and 1¼ cups boiling water in a heatproof bowl. Let steep 5 minutes, then strain through a fine-mesh sieve into a measuring glass (you should have 1 cup). Let cool.
Meanwhile, bring honey, thyme, 2 rosemary sprigs, and ½ cup water to a gentle simmer over medium heat, stirring to combine. Let cool; discard herbs.
Combine tea, honey syrup, lemon juice, bourbon, Cognac, and bitters in a large pitcher or punch bowl. Add 2 cups ice and stir until ice is melted and punch is very cold.
Serve punch in tumblers filled with ice, garnished with rosemary sprigs and lemon wheels.
Do Ahead: Syrup and tea can be made 3 days ahead; cover separately and chill. Punch can be made 1 day ahead; cover and chill.

early-american-eggnog.name: Early American Eggnog
instructions: Add brandy, sherry, rum, and whiskey to a bowl; set aside.

Place egg yolks and egg whites into two separate, large bowls; set egg whites aside. Beat egg yolks with an electric mixer until light and frothy; gradually beat in sugar until light colored. Slowly beat in brandy mixture; beat in milk and heavy cream, with the mixer running, until thoroughly blended. Beat egg whites using a clean beater until soft peaks form; gently fold into milk mixture.
Pour eggnog into two clean, gallon-sized plastic milk containers. Refrigerate 5 to 10 days (10 for a smoother taste). Shake containers occasionally to keep alcohol from separating.
Pour eggnog into a punch bowl; whisk to blend. Add vanilla ice cream; garnish with nutmeg.

earth-style-sweet-potato-sorrels-telecul.name: Earth-style Sweet Potato Sorrels
earth-style-sweet-potato-sorrels-telecul.original_name: Azevias de batata-doce à moda da terra
instructions: Prepare the filling: peel the potatoes, cut them into pieces and bake them in water with cinnamon stick and salt. Remove from the fire, discard the cinnamon, drain the potatoes and reduce the mashed potatoes. Mix the egg, yolk, butter, milk, sugar and lemon zest, stirring between each addition. Take it back to the fire, until it rises boiling, withdraw and reserve.

Prepare the dough: melt the lard and butter. Wipe the flour into a bowl, add a pinch of salt and form a hole in the middle. Put the eggs in it and then the melted fats and mix it with the flour. Add the brandy and knead well. Form a ball and tape with adherent film. Let the dough rest for 30 minutes in the fridge.

After this time, extend the mass on a smooth and floury surface until it is very thin. Distribute portions of filling, with the help of a teaspoon, and tape with the dough. Use a rim or cutter (about 9 cm in diameter) to cut the mistletoe into half-moons, as if they were rissols. Press your extremities tight.

Fry the sourberries in hot oil until they get color on both sides. Remove with a skimmer and let it run on absorbent paper. Mix some sugar with cinnamon powder, sprinkle the sourberries and serve them warm or cold.

easiest-air-fryer-cinnamon-rolls.name: Easiest Air Fryer Cinnamon Rolls
instructions: Stir together the flour, yogurt, and granulated sugar in a large bowl until evenly combined and a soft dough forms. Transfer dough to a lightly floured work surface. Knead gently for 3–5 minutes, just until the dough becomes smooth and pliable, adding more flour 1 tablespoon at a time if the dough is too sticky.
Dust the dough surface again with flour, and roll the dough out into a rough rectangle about 12x16 inches.

Using a rubber spatula, stir together the butter, brown sugar, and cinnamon in a medium bowl until evenly combined. Using an offset spatula or a butter knife, spread the cinnamon sugar mixture evenly across the dough all the way to the edges.
Starting from a short edge, roll the dough up tightly. Trim off the uneven outer ends, then cut the log evenly into 7 (1½-inch) pieces.
Preheat your air fryer to 177°C. Coat a 7-inch round baking dish with cooking spray. Arrange the cinnamon rolls in the prepared baking dish with 1 roll in the center and 6 rolls around the sides.
Place the baking dish in the air fryer basket, and cook for 7–10 minutes, until the tops are golden and the centers are cooked through.
Stir together the powdered sugar, cream cheese, milk, and vanilla in a medium bowl until smooth.
While still warm, drizzle the glaze over the tops of the rolls. Let cool slightly, about 5 minutes. Serve.
Enjoy!

easiest-caramel-apple-clafoutis.name: Easiest Caramel Apple Clafoutis

ingredients.prep: such as Pink Lady or Granny Smith (about 2 pounds total)

instructions: Arrange a rack in center of oven; preheat to 400°. Make the batter first because it needs to rest for a few minutes. Whisk 4 eggs and ½ cup brown sugar in a large bowl until sugar is dissolved and mixture is completely smooth, about 30 seconds. Whisk in ⅓ cup flour and ½ tsp. salt until smooth, then stream in 1½ cups half-and-half and whisk until mixture is homogenous. Add 2 Tbsp. whiskey and 2 tsp. vanilla and mix once just to combine; set aside.

Prepare your apples: Using a vegetable peeler, remove all of the peel, then stand apples upright and slice down along core, working all the way around to remove big lobes of flesh. Cut lobes lengthwise into 1"-thick slices. Discard peel and cores; set aside slices.

Combine 5 Tbsp. butter, remaining ⅓ cup brown sugar, and 3 Tbsp. water in a large heatproof skillet (cast iron is ideal, but you can use any stainless steel skillet that can go in the oven—no plastic handles!). Heat over medium, stirring, until butter is melted and sugar is dissolved. Increase heat to medium-high and bring mixture to a boil. Cook, without stirring but occasionally swirling skillet, until toffee is thickened and bubbles are slightly slower to pop, about 2 minutes.

Add apple slices to skillet and cook, shaking or stirring to toss apples occasionally, until just tender and golden across some surfaces, 10–15 minutes. Remove skillet from heat and scrape apple toffee mixture into a large heatproof bowl.

Rinse skillet under cool water to remove any toffee residue, but don’t bother to wash it. Dry skillet well, then use remaining 1 Tbsp. butter to coat bottom and sides in a thin layer. Dust buttered surfaces with powdered sugar and tap out excess.
Mix batter once more, then pour into skillet. Using a spider or slotted spoon, lift apple slices from bowl, leaving toffee behind, and arrange across bottom of skillet.

Bake custard until batter is puffed and deep golden brown around the edges and golden in the center, 25–30 minutes. Carefully remove from oven (handle will be hot!) and let cool. Custard will look puffy but will fall as it cools.
Let rest at least 15 minutes before serving. Dust with more powdered sugar and drizzle with reserved toffee sauce before serving.
Do Ahead: Custard can be baked 8 hours ahead. Cover and chill.

easiest-chicken-adobo.name: Easiest Chicken Adobo

ingredients.item: Green chiles (such as serrano or jalapeño)

ingredients.prep: skin-on chicken thighs and 4 drumsticks, or 4 whole chicken legs

instructions: Without separating or peeling the cloves, cut entire head of garlic in half through the “equator” (it’s fine if some cloves become detached). Thinly slice 1 green chile, removing seeds if desired for less heat.

If you got whole chicken legs (if not, go to the next step), cover surface of cutting board with a double layer of plastic wrap and place legs skin side up on top (this is to protect your board from any possible contamination; if you have a cutting board that you use for raw meat, use that). You’re going to split the legs into thighs and drumsticks. Working one leg at at time, wiggle drumstick so you can see the joint connecting the thigh and drumstick. Slice down with your knife crosswise between the two, aiming for the joint, until you hit bone. Grasp leg with thigh in one hand and drumstick in the other and break the joint, then cut the rest of the way through with your knife. Repeat with remaining legs.

Heat 1 Tbsp. vegetable oil in a large Dutch oven over medium. Add chicken skin side down and cook, lifting pieces with tongs once or twice toward the end to let hot fat flow underneath, until fat is rendered and skin is crisp and golden brown, 7–10 minutes. Transfer skin side up to a plate. Because drumsticks are covered entirely in skin, you can brown both sides, but it’s not necessary.

Add ¾ cup vinegar, ¾ cup soy sauce, 1 Tbsp. brown sugar, 5 bay leaves, and reserved garlic and sliced chile to Dutch oven. Bring to a simmer, then reduce heat to low, stirring to dissolve sugar. Season generously with pepper.
→  Wait a second, what is white vinegar, anyway?

Return chicken to pot skin side up. Cover and cook chicken very gently, adjusting heat to maintain a bare simmer and turning pieces once, until meat is very tender and pulling away from bone (but not so tender that it’s falling apart), 35–40 minutes.
While chicken is simmering, cook 1 cup rice in medium pot according to package directions.

Transfer chicken to a clean plate. Increase heat to medium-high and boil braising liquid, shaking pot often, until liquid has formed a rich sauce thick enough to coat the back of a spoon, about 5 minutes. Remove pot from heat and return chicken to pot, turning to coat in sauce.
Serve chicken and sauce over rice. Thinly slice remaining chile and scatter over, then season with more pepper.

easiest-chocolate-birthday-cake.name: Easiest Chocolate Birthday Cake

ingredients.item: . semisweet chocolate chips or coarsely chopped chocolate bars

instructions: Place a rack in center of oven; preheat to 325°. Using your fingertips, grease bottom and sides of a 9" round cake pan with vegetable oil. Line bottom of pan with parchment paper, smoothing to eliminate air bubbles. (It may sound fancy, but making a parchment circle is easy! Place pan on a piece of parchment, trace circumference with a pencil, then cut it out.)

Whisk 1⅓ cups all-purpose flour, 1¼ cups granulated sugar, 1¾ tsp. baking powder, ¼ tsp. baking soda, and 1¼ tsp. kosher salt in a large bowl. Sift ½ cup cocoa powder into bowl, then whisk to combine. (You can stop here and package these dry ingredients in an airtight container to create a sort of DIY cake mix if you want!)

Whisk 2 large eggs, ¾ cup vegetable oil, and 1 tsp. vanilla extract in a medium bowl until well combined and creamy, about 30 seconds. Using a spatula, mix egg mixture into dry ingredients until fully incorporated. It will be thick and pasty, that’s okay!

Warm 1 cup milk in a small saucepan over medium heat until it comes to just a bare simmer—make sure it doesn’t come to a boil. Whisk warmed milk into batter until lump-free and just combined; do not overmix.
Immediately transfer batter to prepared pan. Tap pan a few times on work surface to encourage some of the bubbles to escape.
Bake cake, turning pan halfway through, until a cake tester (or toothpick!) inserted into the center of cake comes out clean, 40–50 minutes. Transfer to a wire rack and let cake cool in pan.

Meanwhile, make the frosting: Fill a medium pot with 1" water and bring to a bare simmer over medium heat. Combine 4 oz. chocolate chips and ½ cup heavy cream in a heatproof medium bowl, place over saucepan, and reduce heat to low (make sure bottom of bowl is not touching water). Stir occasionally with a spatula until chocolate is melted and mixture is homogeneous and thick. Chill ganache until cool to the touch, about 25 minutes (you can mix with a spatula to encourage cooling). Alternately, mix chocolate and cream in a microwave-safe bowl and microwave in 10-second bursts until melted.

Using an electric mixer on medium-high speed, whisk 4 oz. (room temperature!) cream cheese, ¼ cup powdered sugar, and remaining ¾ tsp. vanilla extract and ¼ tsp. kosher salt in a large bowl until very smooth and no lumps remain. Beat in cooled chocolate ganache on medium speed, scraping down sides of bowl as needed, until frosting just holds its peak and is well combined and a little thicker, about 1 minute. You don’t want to overbeat because the frosting can become too stiff.
Run a butter knife around edges of pan. Invert cooled cake onto a plate and peel off parchment. Invert again onto a platter.

Top cake with frosting, spreading evenly across the surface. Make some decorative swooshes with a spoon, if desired. Slice and serve.


Editor’s note: Interested in an egg- and dairy-free version? Head this way for a Vegan Chocolate Cake With Chocolate-Orange Frosting →

easiest-corn-on-the-cob.name: Easiest Corn On The Cob

ingredients.item: Ear fresh corn in the husk

instructions: Microwave ear of corn on high until cooked through and tender, about 3 1/2 minutes.
Transfer ear to a cutting board. Hold the tapered end with a towel and cut 1 inch off the bottom of the thick end. Squeeze the tapered end until the ear comes out of the husk and silk.
Spread butter over corn and season with salt.

easiest-cranberry-sauce.name: Easiest Cranberry Sauce
instructions:
Cook 1 (12-oz.) bag fresh or frozen cranberries, ¾ cup granulated sugar, 1–2 Tbsp. Grand Marnier, Cointreau, or other orange liqueur (if using), 1 Tbsp. finely grated orange zest, a pinch of kosher salt, and 1 cup water in a medium saucepan over medium heat, stirring often to keep sauce from sticking to bottom of pan and burning, until cranberries start to burst and become jammy (sauce should coat a spoon), about 10 minutes. Let cool before serving.
Do ahead: Cranberry sauce can be made 5 days ahead. Transfer to an airtight container; cover and refrigerate until well chilled.
Editor’s note: This easy cranberry sauce recipe was first printed on our website in an article titled “Cranberry Sauce Is the Last Thing You Should Stress About on Thanksgiving.” Head this way for more of our best Thanksgiving recipes →

easiest-lamb-gyros-with-creamy-yogurt-sa.name: Easiest Lamb Gyros With Creamy Yogurt Sauce

ingredients.item: Diamond crystal or  0.75 teaspoon morton kosher salt

instructions: You know when you hear chefs talking about “mise en place,” or the process of prepping all your ingredients? Well, that’s what you’re about to do. Cut 1 lemon into wedges and slice off white strip at the point of each wedge, also cutting out seeds (discard white part and seeds). Set wedges aside. Separate leaves of 1 romaine heart and wash/dry if necessary. Tear any large leaves into pieces. Strip oregano leaves from stems; discard stems. Set aside 1 Tbsp. oregano leaves. Finely chop the rest and transfer to a large bowl. Cut 1 cup cherry tomatoes in half.
Finely grate 2 garlic cloves into bowl with chopped oregano. Add 1 tsp. salt, several dashes of hot sauce, and 1 Tbsp. cold water.

Add 1 lb. lamb and use 1 clean hand to knead mixture, simultaneously rotating bowl with your other hand, to incorporate. Knead until a light film forms on side of bowl and mixture holds together in a single mass and is very firm (it will spring back when pressed), about 1 minute.

Heat 3 Tbsp. oil in a large skillet, preferably cast iron, over medium-high until oil is shimmering. Carefully add lamb mixture and press into a single flat layer with a flexible spatula (a fish spatula works great for this).
→  Real Talk: A fish spatula is the only spatula you need.

Cook meat, undisturbed, until underside is browned and crisp around the edges, 5–7 minutes. Watch out for splatter and reduce heat slightly if mixture is splattering a lot.
→  The secret to browning meat so it's crispy and delicious.
Using spatula, break patty into smaller pieces and turn. Cook pieces on the other side until edges are crisp and meat is cooked through, about 5 minutes.
Remove skillet from heat and transfer lamb to a clean large bowl with spatula, then break up meat into small pieces with end of spatula.

Pour off all but a thin layer of fat from skillet into a small heatproof bowl and reserve. Return skillet to medium heat. Working one or two pitas at a time (depending on size of pitas and skillet), cook pitas until underside is golden and crisp around the edges, 1–2 minutes. You only want to crisp 1 side. Transfer pitas to cutting board crisped side down. Add an extra splash of reserved fat to skillet and repeat with remaining pitas.
Finely grate remaining garlic clove into a small bowl and add ¾ cup yogurt and remaining 1 Tbsp. oil. Season with salt and squeeze 2 or 3 lemon wedges into bowl. Stir with a spoon to combine.

Time to build your gyros: Arrange a layer of lettuce leaves down center of each pita (don’t feel like you have to use all the lettuce). Spoon crispy lamb over lettuce, dividing entire mixture evenly. Top with tomatoes, also dividing evenly. Drizzle yogurt sauce over. Dust with an extra sprinkle of salt, then top with reserved oregano leaves. Add hot sauce to taste and serve with remaining lemon wedges alongside.

easiest-meatballs.name: Easiest Meatballs

ingredients.item: Diamond crystal or  0.75 tsp. morton kosher salt

ingredients.prep: finely grated (about  0.5 cup), plus more for serving

ingredients.item: . ground beef (20% fat) or other ground meat
Warm homemade tomato sauce or store-bought marinara and torn basil leaves

instructions: Place rack in top third of oven; preheat oven to 425°. Lightly brush a large rimmed baking sheet with extra-virgin olive oil. Using a sturdy wooden spoon, vigorously stir 2 large eggs, 1½ cup (102 g) panko, ½ cup whole milk, 1½ tsp. Diamond Crystal or ¾ tsp. Morton kosher salt, 1 tsp. garlic powder, 1 tsp. oregano, 2 oz. Parmesan, finely grated (about ½ cup), remaining 3 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil, and several cranks of freshly ground black pepper in a large bowl until nearly a smooth paste. Let the mixture rest for 10 minutes. Mix in a quarter of 1 lb. ground beef (20% fat) or other ground meat (combining just a small amount of meat first makes it easier to incorporate the rest without overmixing). Add remaining ground beef and mix well to thoroughly combine, but don’t overwork it. Using oiled hands, divide meat mixture into eighths; form into 8 large meatballs and place on prepared baking sheet.

Bake meatballs until well-browned underneath, about 15 minutes. Using a stiff metal spatula, pry up and turn over meatballs (they may want to stick a bit). Bake until browned on second side and an instant-read thermometer inserted into the center of each one registers 160°, 5–7 minutes.

Divide meatballs among plates and spoon warm tomato sauce over. Top with torn basil leaves and more Parmesan.
Do Ahead: Meatballs can be baked 3 days ahead. Cool, then chill; reheat in an oven set to 325° or simmer in sauce until warmed through, 10–15 minutes.

Editor’s note: This meatball recipe was first printed in our September 2020 issue as “Weeknight Meatballs.” Head this way for more of our best Italian recipes →

easiest-stir-fry-noodles.name: Easiest Stir-fry Noodles
instructions: Cook noodles according to package directions. Once cooked. Drain and add oil to prevent noodles from sticking together.
In a wok on medium-heat, saute garlic until fragrant. Then, add onions, bell pepper, and green onions and saute until fried and golden brown.
Add soy sauce and vinegar. Then, add noodles and mix well to combine.
Season to taste and serve.

easiest-vanilla-cake.name: Easiest Vanilla Cake

ingredients.item: Diamond crystal or 1 0.25 tsp. morton kosher salt
. diamond crystal or  0.5 tsp. morton kosher salt
. vanilla bean paste or vanilla extract

instructions: Place a rack in middle of oven; preheat to 350°. Grease bottom of a 9"-diameter cake pan with oil and line with a parchment paper round. Grease parchment paper and lightly dust with flour, tapping out excess.

Whisk 1¾ cups (219 g) all-purpose flour, 1 cup (200 g) granulated sugar, 1¾ tsp. Diamond Crystal or 1¼ tsp. Morton kosher salt, 1½ tsp. baking powder, and ½ tsp. baking soda in a medium bowl to combine.

Whisk 2 large eggs, ¾ cup sour cream, ⅔ cup vegetable oil, 2 Tbsp. fresh lemon juice, 1 Tbsp. vanilla extract, and ¼ tsp. almond extract in a large bowl until combined and smooth. Add dry ingredients all at once and gently fold in with a rubber spatula just until incorporated and batter is smooth (do not overmix).

Scrape batter into prepared pan and spread evenly. Bake cake until golden and a tester inserted into the center comes out clean, 30–35 minutes. Transfer pan to a wire rack and let cake cool in pan 30 minutes. Invert cake onto rack and unmold; carefully remove parchment paper. Let cool completely.

While the cake is cooling, using an electric mixer on medium speed, beat 2 oz. cream cheese, room temperature, and ¾ cup (1½ sticks) unsalted butter, room temperature, until fluffy, about 2 minutes. Stop mixer and add 1 cup (110 g) powdered sugar, sifted, 1 tsp. Diamond Crystal or ½ tsp. Morton kosher salt, 1 tsp. fresh lemon juice, and 1 tsp. vanilla bean paste or vanilla extract. Beat on low speed until incorporated, about 1 minute. Beat in remaining 1 cup (110 g) powdered sugar, sifted, until incorporated and frosting is lump-free, about 1 minute.

Place cake, right side up, on a cake stand or large plate. Mound frosting on top and spread over surface, leaving a ½" border around the edges. Using an offset spatula or a large spoon, make decorative swirls, swoops, and divots in frosting if desired. Top with sprinkles (if using).


Editor’s note: Head this way for more of our best cake recipes →

easiest-ever-veggie-burgers.name: Easiest-ever Veggie Burgers

ingredients.item: . diamond crystal or 2 tsp. morton kosher salt
Cooked quinoa (we prefer red or black)

ingredients.prep: pickled onions, tomato, lettuce, pickles, and chipotle yogurt (for serving)

instructions: Prepare a grill for medium-high, direct heat. Clean and oil grate. Shred 1 14-oz. block extra-firm tofu, drained using the large holes of a box grater. Firmly squeeze tofu in between paper towels to drain as much excess liquid as possible.

Heat 2 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium. Cook tofu and 2 14.5-oz. cans black beans, rinsed, drained well, tossing occasionally, until beans look slightly dried out and their skins are darkened, 10–12 minutes.

Meanwhile, whisk ¼ cup almond butter and ¼ cup mayonnaise (vegan, if you are vegan) in a medium bowl until smooth. Whisk in 2 garlic cloves, finely grated, 4 tsp. chili powder (make sure it’s salt-free), and 4 tsp. Diamond Crystal or 2 tsp. Morton kosher salt. Add tofu-bean mixture and 2 cups cooked quinoa (we prefer red or black), cooled and stir well to combine.
Stir ¼ cup flaxseed meal and ¼ cup cold water in a small bowl. Let sit until a thick paste forms, about 1 minute. Add to tofu-bean mixture along with 2 Tbsp. cornstarch and stir well to combine.

Using oiled hands, form mixture into 8–10 patties about ½" thick (about a scant ¾ cup each). Grill until lightly charred around the edges, about 2 minutes. Turn carefully using a metal spatula to cleanly lift off grate and grill until lightly charred on second side and warmed through, about 2 minutes more.

Place burgers on bottoms of toasted buns. Pile high with pickled onions, tomato, lettuce, pickles, and chipotle yogurt. Close with top bun and serve.
Do ahead: Patties can be formed 3 days ahead. Tightly wrap each in plastic and chill. 

Editor’s note: This recipe was first published in June 2018. Head this way for more of our favorite vegetarian recipes →

easily-garlic-herb-roasted-potatoes.name: Easily Garlic Herb Roasted Potatoes
instructions: Fill a large pot with water and boil. Add salt, baking soda, and potatoes, and stir.

Return to a boil, reduce to a simmer, and cook for 10 minutes after returning to a boil. A fork should easily pierce the potato pieces by the end. Drain and let sit one minute while moisture evaporates.

Combine olive oil, rosemary (or thyme), garlic, and a few grinds of black pepper in a small saucepan and heat over medium heat. Stir constantly until garlic just begins to turn golden, about 2-3 minutes.
Immediately strain oil through a fine-mesh strainer over the potatoes. Add salt and pepper to taste, and mix.
Shake the pot that the potatoes are in so that they slam against all sides of the pot, this will loosen the outer layer of the potatoes to form a mashed layer around each potato piece.
Preheat oven to 218°C (220°C).
Transfer potatoes to a large rimmed baking sheet and separate them, spreading them out evenly.
Transfer to oven and roast, without moving, for 30-35 minutes.
Using a thin, flexible metal spatula flip potatoes. Continue roasting until potatoes are deep brown and crisp all over, turning and shaking them a few times during cooking, 30 to 40 minutes longer.
Transfer potatoes to a large bowl and add the garlic-rosemary mixture (optional) and minced parsley. Toss to coat and season with more salt and pepper to taste. Serve immediately.
Enjoy!

easily-stuffed-in-five-minutes-argiro.name: Easily Stuffed In Five Minutes
instructions: Remove the caps from tomatoes and peppers.
Cut the pepper cap – slice and remove the seeds and membranes.
Remove the lid – slice from the tomato and spoon, pour the crumb into a bowl if they have a stalk.

easter-almonds-teleculinaria.name: Easter Almonds
instructions: Lay the ground almond in a bowl, add the sugar and cocoa powder and mix it well.
Then add the light and love everything very well until it becomes a moldable mixture.
Then mold small ''almonds'' with the previous mixture. Pass them through cocoa powder and serve them.

easter-basket-cake.name: Easter Basket Cake
instructions: Preheat the oven to 177°C (175 degrees C). Grease 2 (8-inch) round cake pans. Blend together cake mix, 1 cup water, egg whites, and oil in a large bowl with an electric mixer at low speed until moistened, about 30 seconds. Beat at medium speed for 2 minutes. Pour batter into the prepared cake pans.

Bake in the preheated oven until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean, 23 to 28 minutes. Cool in the pans for 15 minutes. Remove cakes from the pans and cook completely on a wire rack.

Cut a circle out of the center of 1 cake layer, leaving a ring that measures 1 1/2 inches from the outer to the inner edge. Place uncut cake layer on a serving platter and frost the top. Place ring layer on top of frosted layer and frost the sides and top of basket. Decorate the sides and top by pressing candy pieces into frosting.
Combine green food coloring and 1/2 teaspoon water in a medium bowl. Stir in coconut with a fork until evenly tinted. Place green coconut grass in the center of cake basket.

Cut an 8x16-inch piece of aluminum foil. Fold in half lengthwise 4 times. Wrap with ribbon, securing the ends with tape. Insert the ends into the cake top to create a handle. Fill basket with Easter candy and goodies, if desired.

easter-bird-nest-cupcakes.name: Easter Bird Nest Cupcakes
instructions: Preheat the oven to 191°C (190 degrees C). Grease 18 muffin cups or line with paper liners.
Combine 1 cup plus 2 tablespoons flour, 1/2 cup butter, sugar, eggs, and 2 tablespoons cocoa powder in a bowl. Beat with an electric blender until batter is evenly blended.
Spoon batter into the prepared muffin cups, filling each 3/4 full.
Bake in the preheated oven until tops spring back when lightly pressed, 10 to 12 minutes. Cool in the tins for 5 minutes. Transfer to a wire rack and let cool completely, about 20 minutes.

While the cupcakes are cooling, make the buttercream. Beat 3/4 cup butter in a bowl with an electric blender until light and fluffy. Add 1 1/2 cups cocoa powder and confectioners' sugar alternately with 1/2 cup plus 2 tablespoons milk. Add vanilla extract. Beat frosting until it has a spreading consistency, adding more confectioners' sugar or milk as needed.
Transfer chocolate buttercream to a piping bag with a star tip and pipe a round nest onto each cupcake. Sprinkle nest with chocolate sprinkles and place 3 mini chocolate eggs into each nest.

easter-braid-teleculinaria.name: Easter Braid

ingredients.item: 100 g  fios de ovos de compra

instructions: Place the flour on a bench mixed with the pinch of salt and open a cavity in the middle. Heat the 1.5 dl of milk and dissolve the yeast, add to the mixture of flour, also add melted butter and eggs and knead very well until a very homogeneous dough is left. Lay in a bowl sprinkled with flour, cover with a damp cloth and let it leave till it is double the volume.

Lay the dough back on the counter, add the palitated almond, the chopped nuts and the sultans and knead again to wrap the dried fruits. Cut the dough into three portions, extend them a little and then entrance them. Have it on the oven tray sprinkled with flour and let it take another 15 minutes.

Mix the yolk with the 2 tablespoons of milk, brush the braid, add cherries in syrup on top to taste and bring to the oven preheated at 180oC for 30 minutes or until cooked. Stick a toothpick to check, remove it from the oven and let it cool. Spread over the egg threads and serve to taste, for example with colored almonds, as you see in the image. You can sprinkle it with powdered sugar.

easter-bread.name: Easter Bread
instructions: Place 5 or 6 eggs in a medium pot. Pour in cold water to cover by 1 inch and bring to a boil over medium-high heat.
Remove from heat, cover pot, and let stand for 10 minutes.
Transfer eggs to a medium bowl of ice water; let cool completely.

If desired, color eggs according to food-coloring package directions, or check out our recipes for dying your eggs naturally. Transfer eggs to paper towels to dry. (DO AHEAD: Eggs can be dyed up to 1 week ahead. Cover and chill.)
Line a large baking sheet with parchment paper.
Punch down the Master Sweet Dough. Divide into 3 equal pieces.

With lightly floured hands, roll each piece on a lightly floured surface into a 16-inch-long rope with tapered ends. (If dough begins to bounce back, cover with a kitchen towel and let rest for 10 minutes to allow dough to relax.)
Arrange ropes side by side lengthwise on the prepared sheet. Pinch top ends together.
Braid dough. Pinch bottom ends together to secure (braided loaf will be about 12 inches long).
Tuck dyed eggs between braids, spacing evenly.
Loosely cover with plastic wrap or a kitchen towel. Let rise in a warm, draft-free area until puffed but not doubled in size, 45–50 minutes.
Arrange a rack in middle of oven; preheat to 191°C.
Whisk the remaining egg (from the 6-7 total) with 2 tsp. warm water in a small bowl for the egg wash.
Avoiding dyed eggs, brush dough all over with egg wash.
Bake until bread is golden and an instant-read thermometer inserted into center of loaf registers 88°C, 20–25 minutes.
Let cool on a wire rack. Serve warm or at room temperature. (DO AHEAD: Can be made 8 hours ahead. Let cool completely and store airtight at room temperature.)

easter-bread-in-santo-tirso-teleculinari.name: Easter Bread In Santo Tirso
easter-bread-in-santo-tirso-teleculinari.original_name: Broas de Páscoa de Santo Tirso
instructions: Dissolve the yeast in a little warm water. Add 100 g of wheat flour, mix and let it leave a little. Light the remaining water with the sweet grass, spices and salt and let it boil. Wipe the cornmeal into a large bowl and pour the spice water, passed by a passer.

Add the sugar and egg, previously beaten, softened butter and the remaining wheat flour to the dough you were leavening. Wrap with the dough of corn and spices and work on the bench, sprinkled with flour. Let it leave for 3 hours, covered with a cloth.

After the time indicated, mold small balls with your hands and place on a tray, lined with vegetable paper and sprinkled with flour. Let it take a little longer. Then sprinkle it with flour and bring it to the oven, preheated at 190oC, for 15 minutes or until golden.

easter-bun-lezzet.name: Easter Bun

ingredients.item: 1 cup warm milk and water mixture

instructions: 1. Add and melt yeast to warm water and milk mixture.
2. Open the flour dough into the kneading container and the middle of the pool.
3. Mix and mix yeast mixture, sugar, salt, eggs, melted butter and mahlebi.
4. Extract materials and knead until you get a soft dough.
5. Cover the dough with a damp cloth and leave the dough until the two musk roughly.
6. The dough is divided into three equal parts.
7. Prepare roll dough with length of 20 cm.
8. Combine the image and other ends in the form of hair weave by glue from one end.
9. On the egg yolk and sprinkle the fill nuts.
10. Bake 35-40 minutes in the oven with a pre-heated 170 degree.
11. 11. You can serve the Easter pot with banana cake, which is very fast.
12.  Afiyet olsun.
12. August What are the Tricks of Easter Crochet? Wait until it comes to butter and egg to room temperature before starting the cheer.
14 July This will be easier blended with other materials.
15. To be pofuduk of the child, you can add a teaspoon of baking powder and carbonate.
16. If the consistency is soft, you can shape by making flour supplements.
17. September After you prepared the dough, listen to more than 20 minutes.
18. If you wish, you can decorate the ingredients such as sausages and salam by changing the shape.
19. September 1 How many Calorie of the Porsion Easter Chauff?A portion is a rusty bump, about 315 calories.
20. Because oil value is high, it is recommended to consume those with small portions in weight control.

easter-bunny-butt-cake.name: Easter Bunny 'butt' Cake

ingredients.prep: such as General Mills® Fruit Roll-Ups

instructions: Preheat oven to 177°C (175 degrees C). Grease and flour 3 muffin cups. Grease and flour an oven-proof glass or ceramic bowl.

Beat cake mix, water, vegetable oil, and eggs together in a bowl with an electric mixer on low speed until just-moistened. Increase mixer speed to medium and beat until batter is smooth, about 2 minutes. Fill prepared muffin cups with batter; pour remaining batter into prepared oven-proof bowl.

Bake cupcakes in the preheated oven until golden and the tops spring back when lightly pressed, 18 to 21 minutes. Let cupcakes cool briefly in the muffin tin before transferring to a wire rack to cool completely.

Bake cake in the preheated oven until golden and a skewer inserted into the center of the cake comes out clean, 33 to 45 minutes. Let cake rest in the bowl for 10 to 15 minutes before transferring to a wire rack to cool completely. Transfer cooled cake to a serving platter.
Place 2/3 of the coconut into a resealable plastic bag; add red food coloring, seal the bag, and shake until coconut is shaded pink. Repeat process until coconut is your desired shade of pink.

Measure out about 1/2 cup of remaining coconut and place in a bowl. Pour remaining coconut into another resealable plastic bag; add green food coloring, seal the bag, and shake until coconut is shaded green. Repeat process until coconut is your desired shade of green.

Secure 2 cupcakes, tops against the cake, with toothpicks to one end of the cake to form bunny 'feet.' Place 2 marshmallow halves, cut-side down, on top of each cupcake to complete each 'foot.' Secure remaining cupcake, top-side against the cake, with a toothpick to the top of the cake to create the 'tail.'
Spread pink frosting over cake and 'feet', excluding the 'tail.' Cover pink frosting with pink coconut. Spread white frosting over the 'tail' and cover with reserved white coconut.

Cut fruit roll to form 2 small ovals and 6 smaller circles. Using frosting as 'glue,' secure 1 oval and 3 circles to each 'foot' to resemble foot pads. Secure paper bunny ears to the serving plate at the front of the bunny cake using frosting. Sprinkle green coconut around the cake to resemble grass.

easter-bunny-basket-cake.name: Easter Bunny Basket Cake
instructions: Make the chocolate eggs: In a medium microwave-safe bowl, add the dark blue candy melts and 2 teaspoons coconut oil. Melt in the microwave in 30-second intervals, stirring between, until smooth.
Use a fork to drizzle flecks of the melted dark blue chocolate into the cavity of 4 egg-shaped chocolate molds. Place in the freezer for 10-15 minutes, until firm.

Meanwhile, in another medium microwave-safe bowl, add the light blue candy melts and the remaining 1 teaspoon coconut oil. Melt in the microwave in 30-second intervals, stirring between, until smooth.

Spoon a dollop of the melted light blue chocolate into each cavity of the chocolate molds, then spread in an even layer all around the molds, leaving the centers hollow. Place in the freezer for about 30 minutes, or until solid.

Remove 2 cylinders at a time from the molds. Fill 1 cylinder with some of the Funfetti® sprinkles (from the lid of the frosting tub). Dip the edges of the other cylinder lightly in the melted light blue chocolate, then place on top of the sprinkle-filled cylinder. Place on a baking sheet and repeat with the remaining 2 cylinders. Place the chocolate eggs in the freezer for 2 minutes, or until set.

Make the magical cherry: Fill a medium bowl with cold water and add the gelatin sheets. Let sit for 5 minutes, then wring out excess water and place in a large bowl. Add the boiling water and Jell-O mix and whisk together until gelatin is dissolved. Pour the mixture into a silicone cherry-shaped mold and refrigerate for 4-6 hours, until firm.
Unmold the Jell-O cherry and brush with the edible glitter. Cut the Tootsie roll into quarters and shape into stems. Place the stems on top of the Jell-O cherry and set aside.

Make the fruit streamers: Wrap each fruit strip around a thick straw, cover with plastic wrap, and let sit for 30-60 minutes, until they hold their shape. Carefully remove the fruit spirals from the straws and set aside.
Make the cake: Preheat the oven to 177°C (180°C). Grease 4 (8-inch) round cake pans with nonstick spray.

In a large bowl, beat together the Pillsbury™ Funfetti® cake mix, water, eggs, and oil with an electric mixer on medium speed for 2 minutes. Pour the batter into the prepared cake pans. Bake for 34-38 minutes, until a toothpick inserted into the centers of the cakes comes out clean. Let sit for 10-15 minutes before removing to a wire rack to cool completely.

Assemble the cake: Using a large serrated knife, slice a thin layer off the top of each cake layer to create a flat surface; discard the scraps. Smear a bit of the Pillsbury™ Funfetti® frosting onto an 8-inch cardboard cake round set on top of a cake stand, then place a cake layer on the round. Use a large offset spatula to spread an even layer of frosting over the cake. Place another cake layer on top and frost. Repeat with the remaining 2 cake layers, placing the last cake layer on top, bottom-side up. Spread a thin layer of frosting over the top and sides of the cake to make the crumb coat. Refrigerate for about 30 minutes, or until the frosting is set.

Frost the cake: Fit a pastry bag with a basket weave tip and fill with frosting. Pipe a basket weave design on the sides of the cake, leaving the top untouched. Refrigerate for about 30 minutes, or until the frosting is set.
Decorate the cake: Add edible grass to the top of the cake. Decorate with the Jell-O cherry, 2 chocolate eggs, jelly beans, fruit streamers, and the remaining Funfetti® sprinkles.
Thread the marshmallow rope onto skewers and shape into a handle. Place on top of the cake, and enjoy!

easter-bunny-buns-quarkhasen-pequereceta.name: Easter Bunny Buns (quarkhasen)

ingredients.item: 250 g de queso quark o queso fresco batido
1 sobre de levadura en polvo o polvo de hornear (16 g)
Cortador con forma de conejo de pascuacortador con forma de conejos

instructions: Mix quark cheese or bawden cheese cream, milk, sunflower oil, sugar and salt with a bawder to get a soft mass. In another container, mix flour and yeast or baking powder, then add them to the quark cheese mixture. Then mix them up.
Put the mass onto a pitted surface and amase with your hands up to a homogenous mass.
Extiende la masa hasta que tenga un grosor de 1-1,5 cm y corta los conejos con la ayuda de un cortador de conejos (nosotros hemos elegido este modelo).
Luego, coloca los conejitos de queso en una bandeja para hornear forrada con papel de hornear. Bate un huevo con una cucharadita de leche y pincela los conejitos con esta mezcla.
Hornite the Easter Bunnies with a pre-heated oven at 180 ″ 3 ″ (with heat up and down) for 15 minutes.
Once they're ready, put up the cheesebies still hot with butter and then put them up for sugar.

easter-bunny-easter-bread.name: Easter Bunny Easter Bread
instructions: Whisk warm milk, 1/4 cup flour, and yeast for dough together in the bowl of a stand mixer. Let sit about 15 minutes to ensure yeast is active; small bubbles should start to rise to the surface. Add sugar, beaten egg, lemon and orange zest, cinnamon, salt, cardamom, nutmeg, melted butter, and most of the remaining flour (you might not need it all). Mix with a dough hook attachment until dough pulls away from the sides of the bowl and becomes slightly elastic, 5 or 6 minutes. Continue kneading until dough is soft and shiny, about 10 minutes. Remove dough from dough hook and shape into a ball. Transfer to a lightly floured work surface.
Shape dough into a round ball. Transfer to lightly oiled mixing bowl. Cover and let rise in a relatively warm, draft-free place until double in size, about 2 hours.

Poke dough down a bit with your fingertips. Transfer to a lightly floured work surface. Work dough into a uniform piece and cut into eight 4-ounce pieces. Roll one piece with the flats your hands into a 12-inch rope; it's okay if one end is a little thicker than the other.

Cut 2 inches off the smaller end and 3 inches off the larger end. Roll the 2-inch piece into a ball for the tail. Roll the ends of the 3-inch piece into points, then flatten one end slightly for the face. Roll the remaining piece into a 12-inch rope, about 1/2 inch wide.

Transfer the rope to a Silpat®-lined baking sheet and make a coil for the body. The end of the coil will be the bunny's front legs and paws, so place the head on top of that, touching the body, with the flatter side facing the same way as the paws.

Cut the back side of the head (the pointy side) about 2/3 of the way in towards the face to make 2 ears. Make depressions in the middle of each ear with a bench scraper or the back of a knife; do not cut all the way through. Set the tail in place, touching the back of the body.
Repeat to form remaining bunnies; you will use 2 lined baking sheets for 8 bunnies. Cover loosely with plastic wrap and proof in a warm spot for 15 minutes.
Preheat the oven to 204°C (200 degrees C). Beat egg and water together for egg wash.

Remove plastic wrap from the baking sheets and brush dough with egg wash. Poke small holes in the faces for eyes, then push raisins in the holes. Redefine the ears by pressing again with a bench scraper or knife.
Bake in the center of the preheated oven until beautifully browned, 12 to 15 minutes. Carefully remove from the pan and transfer to a wire rack to cool.
Cut into the center of the ears again to redefine them. Brush bunnies with simple syrup and sprinkle coconut onto the tails.

easter-bunny-sweet-bread-for-children-pe.name: Easter Bunny Sweet Bread For Children
easter-bunny-sweet-bread-for-children-pe.original_name: Pan dulce de Conejitos de Pascua para niños

ingredients.item: 500 g de harina de trigo de fuerza (tipo 550) o harina de trigo normal (tipo 405)
1 sobre de levadura seca (12 g) o medio cubo de levadura fresca (25 g)
1 yema de huevo mezclada con 1 cucharada de leche

instructions: Para la masa de levadura, derretimos la mantequilla en el microondas a baja potencia y templamos ligeramente la leche.

Then we mix the flour with the dry yeast into a big bowl. We add the other ingredients: sugar, a pint of salt, egg, melted butter and temperate milk. We amassed everything well by hand till we formed a ball. If the dough stick too much to our hands, we add some more flour.

We cover the bowl with the dough and let them stand at a warm place for at least 30-40 minutes (or more time if we can), for example inside the oven at 50 ″ 3 ″. Meanwhile, we prepared an oven plate with baking paper for later.
When the mass has been Visibly increased, we put it to an enlarged surface, amassed it slightly again and divided into eight portions of the same size.
Formamos con cada una de las ocho porciones un rulo de unos 30 cm de largo.
From each ruble we cut a little bit the size of a nut and form it into a little ball, that will be the rabbit's tail.

Then we double each rulo as "U" and clamp the ends with each other. We leave a small hole at the central bottom to place the ball that makes up the rabbit's tail. We put the little rabbits already formed into the oven plate and leave them with space and let them rest for another 15 minutes.
Finally, we pinch the rabbits with the mixture of yema and milk (1 yema with 1 tablespoon of milk) and we sprinkle with lost sugar.
We put the decorated rabbits into the pressurized oven and baked them for 15-20 minutes at 180-3-o (heat up and down), until they are slightly golden.

easter-buns-from-argyros-argiro.name: Easter Buns From Argyros

ingredients.item: 3 tears of mastic beaten in powder
3 tears of mastic beaten in powder

instructions: We start by activating yeast first. Dissolve it in a bowl, add the sugar and water.
Mix, cover and allow for 5-10 minutes to foam the surface, sample that the yeast is activated for the teas.
If you do not find fresh yeast, you can use 4 dry yeast envelopes that will activate it as I say above.

easter-cake-teleculinaria.name: Easter Cake
instructions: Prepare the cream: Light the milk with vanilla sugar, let it warm and turn off the fire.

In a bowl, mix the egg with the yolks and sugar, add the flour, stir and then add the milk to yarn and always stir. Pass the mixture by the thin network strainer to another pan and bring it to the fire, stirring until boiling and a smooth cream. Lie down for a glass, cover with adherent pelicula and let it cool.

Connect the oven to 180o C. Barre a hole shape with vegetable cream and sprinkle with flour. Prepare the dough: mix the flour with chocolate and peel. Lay the eggs for a glass, add the sugar and beat for 20 minutes. Add the mixture of flour and chocolate and stir gently.

Lay the dough in shape and put it in the oven for 20 minutes. Rectify the cooking with a toothpick, remove, peel and let it cool. After cold, bar the cake with the cream, decorate with chocolate chips and almonds and serve.

easter-cake-in-air-fryer-pequerecetas.name: Easter Cake In Air Fryer
easter-cake-in-air-fryer-pequerecetas.original_name: Mona de Pascua en freidora de aire

ingredients.item: 10 g de levadura fresca o 5 g de levadura seca
40 ml de aceite de oliva suave
Eggs (quantity according to the monkeys we make)
50 g of smoked sugar with a few drops of water

instructions: We put the flour in a big bowl. We add the yeast and mix with a fork to distribute it well. If dry yeast is used, simply add it directly with the flour. If we prefer to use fresh yeast, the ideal will be to crumple and dissolve them earlier into some temperate milk - taken from the total quantity - before adding them to the mixture. Once the yeast has been added, the other ingredients are added: olive oil, milk (or whatever remains of it if a part has been used to dissolve the fresh yeast), sugar, egg, an orange or lemon frying, a pint of azahar water and a pint of salt. It all intersects up to a homogenous and well integrated mass.

We amassed the dough for a few minutes onto a slightly pitted surface, until soft and elastic. If it's too sticky, you can add some more flour to work better. When it has a homogenous texture, we give them a ball shape with his hands, trying to make them smooth and well formed.

We put the dough ball into a slightly ground bowl with some oil and cover with a clean cloth or film paper. We let him rest in a hot place till his size's double. If you are in a hurry or the kitchen's cold, a very useful option is to use the airfryer if you have that option: you can program at about 30-40-3) for about 60 minutes, with only heat and with no fan. The mass can also be put up close to a heat source (as an off but warm radiator, or inside the off oven with light) for about 60 to 90 minutes, depending on room temperature.

As the dough grows, we cover the air fryer basket with baking paper suitable for airfryer. Once the mass has been lifted, we divide it, for instance. in 2 or 4 parts, depending how many "monkeys" we want to do and how big we give them. We form the monkeys as we best like them. In our case, we have chosen to make 2 medium monkeys and give them the classic round shape with a hole in the middle where we have put an egg, but you can give them as you wish. Let the little ones participate and enjoy!

With the monkeys already formed, we paint them with an egg yolk mixed with a tablespoon of milk and let them rise about 20 minutes more covered with a cloth. After 20 minutes we re-paint them with an egg and milk mixture and decorate with smoked sugar. To bake the monkeys into the airfryer, place them in the basket without them touching each other or one at a time depending on their size. Bake them at 165 ″ 3 ″ for about 20 minutes, or till they are golden and cooked from inside. The time may vary by the size of the Easter monkeys, so it's best to review them from time to time.

Once baked, we get them out of the airfryer and let them cool slightly onto a grid. Serve the hot or cold Easter monkeys. They are perfect for family sharing during snack or Easter breakfast. And as the Valencian tradition commands, they can also be given to our godfamilies.

easter-cake-with-topping-teleculinaria.name: Easter Cake With Topping

ingredients.item: 3 vials/boons carrot puree for baby (200 g each)

instructions: Prepare the cake: in a bowl, mix the flour with sugar, bicarbonate, cinnamon and salt. Add the oil, eggs and carrot puree and mix well.
Drain the pineapple, cut it to pieces and crush it with a fork. Add to the previous dough as well as the nuts.

Divide the dough by two equal round shapes, previously greased with butter and sprinkled with flour. Bring it to the oven, preheated at 180oC, for about 35 to 40 minutes, or until the toothpick comes dry. Remove and let it cool for 10 minutes before deforming.
Prepare the cover: in a bowl, beat the cheese with butter until it is homogeneous. Add the sugar and vanilla and beat until thick. Finally, involve a little bit of chopped nut.

After deformed, spread some of the previous cream over the top of one of the cakes. Have the other cake on top and cover them completely with the remaining cream. Decorate with edible flowers and take to the refrigerator for 1 hour. Remove and serve immediately.

easter-cakes-for-children-in-the-shape-o.name: Easter Cakes For Children In The Shape Of Animals
easter-cakes-for-children-in-the-shape-o.original_name: Monas de Pascua para niños con forma de animalitos

ingredients.item: 20 g de levadura fresca o 7 g de levadura seca
80 ml de aceite de oliva suave
Rilling of half an orange or lemon
1 yema de huevo y 1 cucharada de leche para pintar
Eggs (quantity according to the monkeys we make)
Color noodles, chocolate balls, raisins, laminated almonds...

instructions: In a small bowl, we mix 50 g of flour with 50 ml of warm milk and yeast. Let's clear up till we get a dense and sticky mass. We cover the bowl and let them stand at a hot place about 20- 30 minutes, till they are filled with bubbles and we see that they have grown and have become fluffy.

In a big bowl, we put 450 g of flour and 80 g of sugar. We make a hole at the centre and add the liquid ingredients: 80 ml of soft olive oil, 125 ml of tempered milk, 2 eggs, an orange frying and a pint of salt.

We added the startup mass to the main bowl. We first mix with a spoon or fork and, once the ingredients have been integrated, we pass the mass to the counter. We amassed for about 10-12 minutes till the dough stopped being sticky and became smooth and elastic. If you stick to your hands, you better put them with some olive oil rather than add more flour.
We form a ball and place it into a slightly greased bowl. We cover with a cloth and let them stand at a hot place, far from air currents, till they double their volume (between 1.5 and 2 hours).

Once the mass has grown, we divide it into 4 or 5 parts depending on how many monkeys you have to do. We form balls with each of them and we start giving them the form of an animal we want, we have made a turtle, a rabbit and a fish, but you can let your imagination blow up.

For example, for the turtle we get one of the balls and from that we separate another 5 pieces of similar size, for the four legs and head. With the big piece we form the body and brush with the beamed egg mixed with milk to help better stick the legs and head. Then we put an egg at the centre of his body, tighting well, and we let them rest about 45-60 minutes more in a hot place till you see them have grown in size.

With the rest of the balls we form the rest of the animals, for example an Easter rabbit in which we form our ears and legs with pieces of dough, of course you can add an egg. You can let your children help you and you will have a great time.

Una vez las figuras han vuelto a levar por segunda vez, podemos empezar a decorarlas. Para ello, volvemos a pincelar con la mezcla de huevo y leche y decoramos con bolitas de colores, pasas, smarties o almendras laminadas.

We finally baked at 180 ″ 3 ″ for about 20-25 minutes, till they are well golden. Go monitoring the oven as it will depend on the size of the monkeys, but if you see that they are getting too golden you can cover them with half baked aluminum paper. We get them out of the oven, we let them get cold, and if we want we can trade our baked eggs with some hot or chocolate eggs as a décor. That they have been "hundreds"?

easter-cheesecake-argiro.name: Easter Cheesecake

ingredients.item: 10-12 fresh onions with their green leaves
6 eggs separated the yolk from the white
A little olive oil for the baking pan
10-12 fresh onions with their green leaves
6 eggs separated the yolk from the white
A little olive oil for the baking pan

instructions: Wash well and dry all the vegetables we will use for the Easter cheese pie.
Finely chop the pumpkins and the fresh onions separately and then the leek.
In pan heat the olive oil over medium heat and add the leek and fresh onions, allowing them to soften slightly for 10 minutes.
Add the pumpkin and let it wither and absorb its liquids. Remove from heat and allow the vegetables to cool for 5–6 minutes.
In a large bowl, mix the salted vegetables, yolks, milk, flour, baking powder, cheeses, herbs, salt and pepper until homogenized.
In a mixer, beat the whites in meringue until soft peaks are formed (about 2 minutes). Gently add the meringue to the other ingredients and mix with gentle movements so that it doesn't get down.

Pour the mixture into a well oiled long fireproof bowl and bake in a well preheated oven: 170°C in the resistors for 45 minutes, until golden and stabilized, or 160°C in the air on the medium grill for about 35 minutes.
Allow it to cool before cutting into slices and serve.

easter-chicken-teleculinaria.name: Easter Chicken
instructions: Place the chicken on a tray and season with white wine, olive oil, chopped garlic, colorau, rosemary and salt. Take it in the oven, preheated to 180oC, and let it bake for about an hour, or until it gets golden. Water once in a while with your own sauce.
Wash the sarcacene wheat, drain it and cook it in the salted water for about 13 to 15 minutes after the water starts boiling. Remove and reserve.
Descasque e pique grosseiramente a cebola e um pouco de salsa. Corte os tomates em quartos. Junte estes ingredientes ao trigo, depois de cozido, e envolva.
Add to the wheat also the roast sauce, mix and put on a serving dish. Have the chicken on top and serve immediately.

easter-chocobanana-with-whipped-cream-an.name: Easter Chocobanana With Whipped Cream And Bun
easter-chocobanana-with-whipped-cream-an.original_name: Πασχαλινή σοκομπανάνα με τρεμουλιαστή κρέμα και τσουρέκι
instructions: Butter a refractory long-staird vessel and coat the slices.
Butter and surface and spread the banana slices on a layer.
Add cinnamon and black sugar.
Bake in a well preheated oven for 10′ at 200oC in the air.
Beat the eggs and sugar with a mixer and become cream.
Add the milk, cinnamon and salt and beat them together.
Finally, add the chocolate drops to the cream and pour the baked bread.
Sprinkle with nuts and raisins.
We make a layer of the remaining bananas in slices.
Bake for about 25 minutes until the cream has a body, stabilize and slightly golden.
Once removed from oven, sprinkle with powder and cinnamon.
We serve it warm.
When it comes out of the oven the cream will be shivering while it cools.
Warm is great, but when it gets cold cut into pieces, it's fantastic.

easter-chocolate-eggs-made-with-a-mold.name: Easter Chocolate Eggs Made With A Mold
instructions: Place 3/4 of the milk chocolate in the top of a double boiler over simmering water. Stir frequently, scraping down the sides with a rubber spatula to avoid scorching, until chocolate is melted and reaches a temperature of 45°C (46 degrees C), about 5 minutes. Remove from the heat.
Add remaining milk chocolate and stir until smooth and the temperature cools to 30°C (30 degrees C).

Spread one layer of chocolate evenly inside the mold halves using a pastry brush; refrigerate for 10 minutes. Brush on another layer of chocolate and chill for 10 minutes. Brush a third time and chill for a final 10 minutes.

Unmold the egg halves carefully and trim the edges with a sharp knife. Brush the edges with a little melted chocolate, then carefully bring two halves together to form a single egg. Set back in the mold carefully and chill until set, 10 to 15 minutes.

easter-cod-kitchen-robot-teleculinaria.name: Easter Cod (kitchen Robot)
instructions: Place the cod in the steam cooker, add the washed eggs, fit the robot glass with 500 g of water and program at steam temperature (120oC on Mycook) for 25 minutes at speed 2 (speed 3 on Mycook). Then put the eggs in a bowl with cold water, let the cod be crushed and chip it by rejecting the skin and the spines. Book the cooking water.

Peel and wash the onions and cloves of garlic and cut the onions in half fine moons. Lay the garlic on the drained cup, turn 5 seconds at speed 5 (6 seconds and speed 6 on Mycook), add the onion and olive oil and program at 100oC for 5 minutes at minimum speed (speed 2 on Mycook). Squeeze the naked tomatoes with your hand, add them to the cup, add the masena flour and 300 g of cod cooking water, stir the spatula to undo the flour, season with nutmeg, salt and pepper if necessary and program at steam temperature (120oC on Mycook) for 15 minutes at minimum speed (speed 2 on Mycook).

Lay down the previous mixture for a large bowl, add the cod, the straw potato and a little chopped coriander and wrap it very well. Then spread it on a dish tray, alternately with the boiled eggs cut into slices, sprinkle it with grated cheese and bring it to the oven preheated at 220oC until it is golden. Remove from the oven and serve decorated to taste or as you see in the photo.

easter-cod-au-gratin-food-processor-tele.name: Easter Cod Au Gratin - Food Processor
easter-cod-au-gratin-food-processor-tele.original_name: Bacalhau gratinado de Páscoa - robot de cozinha
instructions: Light a frying pan with oil and let it warm. Pass the cod posts by flour, take them to fry on both sides, remove and reserve.

Peel the onions and garlic, cut the onions into half moons and pinch the garlic teeth. Praise them in a frying pan with olive oil, add the finely chopped naked tomato and let it saute until soft. Correct the seasonings, remove from the fire and reserve.
Peel the potatoes and cook them in salted water for 35 minutes. Spill the potatoes, pass them through the pass, add the milk and butter, season with nutmeg and stir well.

Lay the cod posts on a platter and lay the onion on top. Place the mashed mashed potatoes in a pastry bag with frizzy mouth and dispose around the cod. Finally, put the mayonnaise on the cod posts and serve.

easter-cod-casserole-teleculinaria.name: Easter Cod Casserole
instructions: Peel the potatoes, cut them into pieces and bake them in water for 15 minutes. Remove, drain and reserve. Peel the onions and garlic, cut the onions into blades and pick the garlic; clean the pepper and cut into thin strips.

Leve ao lume um tacho com o azeite e os alhos, junte a cebola e o pimento e deixe refogar. Adicione as migas de bacalhau e o leite e deixe cozer, durante 5 minutos. Escorra, aproveitando o caldo, e reserve o bacalhau com o refogado.
Reduce the potatoes to puree and add the cod broth (if necessary, add more milk) and butter. Take it to the fire, stir well until it is homogeneous, retrace the salt and remove it from the fire.

On a board, have half the mashed potatoes, place the cod on top and cover with the remaining mashed potatoes. Brush with the egg beaten and decorate with olives. Take it in the oven, preheated to 200oC, for about 20 minutes. Serve hot.

easter-cod-with-almonds-teleculinaria.name: Easter Cod With Almonds
instructions: Peel and slice the onions and garlic teeth. Spread it on the bottom of a tray, water with a little olive oil and place on top of the peeled chips. Season with salt and light in the oven, preheated at 180oC, for 30 minutes.
However, in a processor, roughly crush the almonds with the parsley and reserve.

After the time indicated, remove the tray from the oven, add the cod and water with plenty of olive oil. Spread over the mixture of almond and parsley and put it back in the oven for another 30 minutes, or until the cod is cooked. Serve right away.

easter-cookie-lezzet.name: Easter Cookie

ingredients.item: 225 gr butter (softened in the heat of the room)

instructions: 1. knead until it comes to egg, butter and powder sugar cream consistency.
2. Add other materials and continue kneading.
3. Remove shapes with dough molds.
4. Cook and cool in oven 170 degrees.
5. Decorate your excess with whipped cream that you color according to your request and listen to a watch.
6. Serve.

easter-cookies-argiro.name: Easter Cookies
instructions: Initially sift the flour with baking and set aside.
Beat the mastic tears with a minimum of sugar until it turns to dust.
Add the soft butter to the mixer's bowl.
Add the sugar and beat at medium speed for 4-5’ until they become cream.
Dig the side bowl with spatula.
Add the zest, mastic powder and one by one the yolks.
Once homogenized, add the cognac.
Dissolve the baking soda in the orange juice and add them to the mixture.
Add the flour and at low speed mix the ingredients very little until dough is formed.
Once the dough is removed from the bowl walls, leave it for 10’ covered.
The dough total weighs 850 grams.
Preheat oven to 160oC.
Cut small pieces of dough, weighing 25-30 grams.
We form in Easter plans.
Add them to a shallow baking pan between them, lined with non-stick paper.
With a brush, brush around the cookies, with some egg solution.
Place the first baking pan in the hot oven on the medium grill.
Bake for about 25′ until golden.
The cooking time is relative from oven to oven, different.
We make the rest.
After they are cooked, let them cool and transfer to airtight bowls after they cool completely, cover and keep them crispy for a long time.

easter-cookies-from-argyros-argiro.name: Easter Cookies From Argyros
instructions: To make cookies crispy, all materials must be at room temperature and butter soft. Shake the flour and baking powder in a bowl.
Beat the butter in the mixer's bowl with the wire for 1 minute, to fluffy.
gradually incorporate the sugar by working the ingredients with the stirring component for 6-7 minutes, until the sugar melts completely and the mixture becomes creamy.
Add the zest, vanilla, mastic and salt.
Add the eggs, one by one, with the mixer always in operation until completely absorbed.
Dissolve the soda in the juice and pour it into the mixture.
Lower the mixer speed and integrate flour with baking powder into doses – it may not take all of it.

In the end we have to have a fluffy, buttery and malleable dough. Since we drop the flour, we don't work too hard because the cookies are going to get tough. Cover it and leave it for 20 minutes to rest.
Preheat the oven to 160°C, in the air.
Beat in a bowl with the fork the ingredients for the solution to homogenize.
We make cookies from the dough by giving them whatever shape we want.
Add them to a baking pan lined with non-stick paper and rub with the egg solution.
Bake the Easter cookies for about 25 minutes, until golden.
Leave them on a grill to cool well and then keep them in an airtight vessel to stay fluffy and crispy until the last.

easter-cookies-from-santo-tirso-teleculi.name: Easter Cookies From Santo Tirso
easter-cookies-from-santo-tirso-teleculi.original_name: Broinhas de Páscoa de Santo Tirso
instructions: Throw in a bowl the flours, butter, egg, water, yeast and a little sweet grass and cinnamon. Knead all ingredients thoroughly until you get a moldable dough. If necessary, add some more water.
Unte dois tabuleiros com manteiga e polvilhe com farinha. Disponha neles colheradas da massa anterior, distanciadas entre si, e pincele-as com a gema previamente misturada com o leite.
Bring the trays to the oven, preheated at 180oC, for about 20 minutes or until the broinhas are cooked and golden. Remove, let it cool and serve.

easter-crown-teleculinaria.name: Easter Crown

ingredients.item: 1  saqueta de fermento de padeiro seco
To decorate powdered sugar and egg yarn

instructions: In a large bowl, dissolve the yeast into warm milk. Then add the sugar, butter, eggs, flour, salt and lemon zest to taste. Knead everything very well until you get a homogeneous mass. Let it light for an hour.

After the time indicated, re-shuffle the mass and extend it in a rectangle. Spread over the pumpkin candy and roll. Join the ends, forming a crown and dispose on a tray, sprinkled with flour. Let it take another 30 minutes.
Then brush with the yolk, previously mixed with milk, and sprinkle with granulated almond. Give some cuts on the board so you can see the stuffing.
Take it in the oven, preheated to 180oC, for about 35 to 40 minutes. Remove it from the oven and let it cool. Sprinkle with powdered sugar, decorate with egg threads and serve.

easter-cupcakes-teleculinaria.name: Easter Cupcakes
instructions: Garnish small shapes with butter and sprinkle them with flour. Start the oven at 180 degrees.
In a bowl, hit the butter well with the sugar. Then add the eggs, one by one and always hitting very well between each addition. Then add the yeast, flour and cocoa and tap it again very well.
Distribute the dough through the shapes and put it in the oven for 20 minutes, or until it is cooked. Remove it from the oven, let it cool down and peel off.
Prepare the cream: Lay the ingredients indicated in a bowl and beat them well. Put the cream in a pastry bag and decorate the top of the cupcakes. Embellish with Easter almonds and serve.

easter-dirt-cake.name: Easter Dirt Cake
instructions: Process cookies and melted butter in the bowl of a food processor until cookies are finely ground and the mixture is thoroughly combined; reserve 1 ½ cups of mixture for topping cake.
Press remaining mixture into a lightly greased (with cooking spray) 9x13-inch baking dish. Chill while preparing filling, about 20 minutes.

Beat softened butter and cream cheese at medium speed with a stand mixer, fitted with the paddle attachment, until creamy, 1 to 2 minutes. Gradually add 1 ¼ cups powdered sugar and beat until very creamy and thoroughly combined, about 1 minute.

Beat pudding mix, milk, and vanilla in a medium bowl with an electric mixer at medium speed for 2 minutes until mixture has thickened. Add pudding mixture to cream cheese mixture and beat at medium speed until very smooth and combined, about 2 minutes.

Beat heavy cream and cream of tartar in a medium bowl with an electric mixer at medium speed until foamy, about 2 minutes; gradually add remaining 1/4 cup powdered sugar and beat on medium-high until medium-stiff peaks form, about 5 to 6 minutes. Gently fold whipped cream into cream cheese-pudding mixture.
Spoon cream cheese-pudding mixture on top of chocolate cookie crumb mixture in baking dish; smooth with a small off-set spatula.
Sprinkle reserved 1 ½ cups chocolate cookie crumb mixture evenly over top.
Cover and chill at least 4 hours, up to 24 hours. Decorate top of cake as desired with desired toppings.

easter-egg-cheese-ball.name: Easter Egg Cheese Ball
instructions: Mix together cream cheese, Cheddar cheese, and dressing mix in a bowl until well combined.
Form into a large egg-shaped ball in the center of a serving plate.

Cut wax paper into strips. Lay them across the cheese ball, leaving spaces every two strips where the cheese is exposed. Sprinkle chopped chives on the exposed cheese, lightly pressing the chives down so they stick to the cheese. Remove the wax paper strips directly below the chives. Cover the newly exposed cheese stripes with bacon bits. Repeat by removing the final set of wax paper strips and adding chopped pecans in the remaining spaces.
Refrigerate for at least 2 hours, or overnight.

easter-eggs.name: Easter Eggs
instructions: Beat together cream cheese, margarine, and vanilla in a large bowl with an electric mixer until well combined. Add confectioners' sugar in batches, beating well after each addition. Dough will be very stiff.
Divide dough equally into 2 bowls. Stir peanut butter into one bowl and coconut into the other until incorporated.
Using your hands, mold dough into egg shapes and arrange them on cookie sheets. Place molded eggs in the freezer until frozen, 1 to 2 hours.
Melt together chocolate chips and shortening on top of a double boiler until smooth. Allow to cool slightly at room temperature.
Dip frozen eggs into melted chocolate mixture until completely coated. Place dipped eggs on wax paper-lined cookie sheets and return to the freezer to harden. Store eggs in the refrigerator.

easter-folar-teleculinaria.name: Easter Folar
instructions: Place the flour on the counter and open a cavity in the center. Dissolve yeast in warm milk and place it in the flour cavity. Add the raw eggs, butter, sugar, spices and a pinch of salt and start mixing everything carefully. Make sure it's all tied up.
Transfer the dough to a bowl, cover it with a cloth and let it leave, until double in size.
Nessa altura, coloque a massa novamente sobre a bancada, divida-a em duas partes iguais e forme duas bolas, achatando-as um pouco. Disponha um ovo cozido no centro de cada uma.

Line a board with vegetable paper and have the folars in it. Take it to the oven, preheated at 170°C, for about 30 minutes. A few minutes before you finish cooking, brush the surface with beaten egg and let it finish. Remove from the oven, let it cool and serve.

easter-goat-stew-kitchen-robot-teleculin.name: Easter Goat Stew (kitchen Robot)
easter-goat-stew-kitchen-robot-teleculin.original_name: Caldeirada de cabrito de Páscoa (robot de cozinha)
